originalTitle: Jim Cramer says it's time to look beyond tech as AI uncertainty rattles the market
summary: CNBC's Jim Cramer says the AI trade has become too unpredictable, making this a better time to put new money into high-quality companies outside of technology.
content:
CNBC's Jim Cramer said Monday that the artificial intelligence trade has become too noisy and unpredictable to aggressively put new money to work — at least for now.

"If you own too much tech, you're going to be slaughtered, and you won't even know what hit you," the " Mad Money " host said. "For the moment, it's time to go to other sectors. They can make you money, without the volatility."

Semiconductor and AI-related stocks have come under pressure in recent weeks after surging to records earlier this year. Cramer argued it may be time to stop chasing every twist in the AI trade and instead focus on high-quality companies in other sectors .

"It makes me want to just find high-quality companies like Goldman Sachs and Wells Fargo , or dig my teeth into FedEx and FedEx Freight , or scoop up some Honeywell and Boeing ," he said. Cramer's Charitable Trust, the portfolio used by the CNBC Investing Club, owns all six companies.

Still, Cramer stressed that he is not abandoning artificial intelligence altogether.

He said he remains bullish on Club name Nvidia , arguing the chipmaker continues to dominate the data center market despite customers' efforts to develop their own chips.

"Nvidia is at the heart of the data center," Cramer said, saying it's AI server racks are "the envy of the world and only AMD comes close."

Cramer also reiterated his bullish stance on Intel ahead of the chipmaker's earnings report , calling it a "triple play" because of its central processing unit (CPU) business, advanced chip-packaging capabilities, and its burgeoning third-party foundry business.

"Intel is a national treasure," said Cramer, who also owns Intel for the Club.

Even so, Cramer said he is waiting for a broader washout in technology before adding meaningfully to the sector. Until then, he thinks investors are better off putting money to work in high-quality companies outside tech while they wait for more attractive entry points into AI stocks.

chineseTitle: 吉姆·克莱默 (吉姆·克莱默) 表示，随着 AI 的不确定性扰乱市场，是时候将目光投向科技以外的领域了
chineseSummary: CNBC 的吉姆·克莱默 (Jim Cramer) 表示，人工智能行业变得过于不可预测，现在是向技术以外的优质公司投入新资金的更好时机。
chineseContent:
CNBC 的吉姆·克莱默 (Jim Cramer) 周一表示，人工智能行业已经变得过于喧闹且难以预测，无法积极投入新资金——至少目前如此。

“如果你拥有太多科技，你就会被屠杀，你甚至不知道是什么打击了你，”《疯狂金钱》主持人说。 “目前，是时候转向其他行业了。它们可以让你赚钱，而且不会出现波动。”

半导体和人工智能相关股票在今年早些时候飙升至创纪录水平后，最近几周面临压力。克莱默认为，现在可能是时候停止追逐人工智能行业的每一个转折点，转而关注其他领域的优质公司了。

他说：“这让我想要寻找像高盛和富国银行这样的优质公司，或者深入研究联邦快递和联邦快递货运，或者收购一些霍尼韦尔和波音。”克莱默慈善信托基金（CNBC 投资俱乐部使用的投资组合）拥有全部六家公司。

尽管如此，克莱默强调，他并没有完全放弃人工智能。

他表示，他仍然看好俱乐部名称英伟达，认为尽管客户努力开发自己的芯片，但这家芯片制造商仍继续主导数据中心市场。

克莱默表示：“英伟达是数据中心的核心。”他表示，英伟达的人工智能服务器机架“令全世界羡慕，只有 AMD 能与之媲美”。

克莱默还在芯片制造商财报发布前重申了他对英特尔的看好立场，称其为“三网合一”，因为其中央处理器（CPU）业务、先进的芯片封装能力以及蓬勃发展的第三方代工业务。

“英特尔是国宝，”俱乐部拥有英特尔的克莱默说道。

即便如此，克莱默表示，他正在等待技术领域出现更广泛的淘汰，然后再对该行业进行有意义的投资。在那之前，他认为投资者最好将资金投入科技以外的优质公司，同时等待更有吸引力的人工智能股票切入点。
